title: Finance Briefing: US Treasury rout tests Washington's tolerance for higher borrowing costs - Reuters
summary: Leo Stone maps the latest market headlines to liquidity, rates, earnings expectations, and risk appetite.

body.text: The useful way to read market news is to connect each headline to a mechanism: cash flows, liquidity, policy expectations, positioning, or risk appetite.

items: US Treasury rout tests Washington's tolerance for higher borrowing costs - Reuters (Reuters)
Soaring stocks could face rocky patch as earnings wind down, yields perk up By Reuters - Investing.com (Investing.com)
Treasury Yields Just Hit a 1-Year High—Here’s How You May Feel It - AOL.com (AOL.com)
The surge in Treasury yields puts these stocks in the spotlight - MSN (MSN)
Treasury Yields Snapshot: May 22, 2026 (null:US10Y) - Seeking Alpha (Seeking Alpha)
Strategists warn: The likelihood of rate hikes by the Federal Reserve and other central banks is increasing, rather than rate cuts. - Bitget (Bitget)

body.text: The first-order story is rarely enough. A professional reading asks whether the news changes the discount rate, the earnings path, the cost of capital, or the market's willingness to pay for uncertainty.
This article is informational analysis, not investment advice. The goal is to make the signal clearer and the assumptions more explicit.
